SEARCH
Package de.dhbw.memory.model

Enum Class Theme

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<Theme>
de.dhbw.memory.model.Theme
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<Theme>, Constable
public enum Theme extends Enum<Theme>
Available card themes. Each theme knows its static-resource folder and the full list of 18 motif names (enough for a 6×6 grid; the first 8 are used for a 4×4 grid).
Author:
Markus Wenninger

  • Method Details

    • values

      public static Theme[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
      Returns:
      an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared

    • valueOf

      public static Theme valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null

    • getFolder

      public String getFolder()
      Returns the static-resource sub-folder name for this theme (e.g. "crypto"). Images live at src/main/resources/static/images/<folder>/<motif>.svg (served at /images/<folder>/<motif>.svg).

    • getMotifsFor

      public List<String> getMotifsFor(int gridSize)
      Returns the first (gridSize * gridSize) / 2 motif names for this theme. For a 4×4 grid that is 8 motifs; for a 6×6 grid it is 18.
      Parameters:
      gridSize - either 4 or 6
      Returns:
      immutable sub-list of motif names
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if gridSize is not 4 or 6

    • getDisplayName

      public String getDisplayName(String motif)
      Returns the human-readable display name for a motif id (e.g. "btc" → "Bitcoin"). Used by the view to render a friendly label on each card. If the motif is unknown, the id is returned as-is so we never crash on a missing entry.
      Parameters:
      motif - the motif id from getMotifsFor(int)
      Returns:
      the display name suitable for showing to the user
